steps.title: Calculate the slope using two known points
steps.content:
The line passes through $(7, 1)$ and $(0, 4)$. The slope is:
$$m = \frac{4 - 1}{0 - 7} = \frac{3}{-7} = -\frac{3}{7}$$

steps.title: Find the x-intercept using the slope-intercept form
steps.content:
Since $(0, 4)$ is the y-intercept, the equation is $y = -\frac{3}{7}x + 4$. The point $(c, 0)$ lies on this line, so substitute $y = 0$:
$$0 = -\frac{3}{7}c + 4$$
$$\frac{3}{7}c = 4$$
$$c = 4 \cdot \frac{7}{3} = \frac{28}{3}$$

Equivalent forms accepted: 28/3, 9.333.
